Induced Damage  Experimental Animal  Intervention  Duration  Main Outcomes a  Reference
Obesity-related inflammation  C57BL/6 J mice  5% Cocoa-enriched diet (50 mg flavanols/Kg.b.w.)  18 weeks  ↓ High fat diet-induced IL-6 and expression of pro-inflammatory genes  [56]
Adipose tissue inflammation  C57BL/6 J mice  5% Cocoa-enriched diet (50 mg flavanols/Kg.b.w.)  18 weeks  ↓ High fat diet-induced TNF-α, IL-6, iNOS, NF-κB, COX-2  [57]
Adipose tissue inflammation  Swiss mice  10% Cocoa-enriched diet (50 mg flavanols/Kg.b.w.)  4 weeks  ↑ High fat diet-down regulated PPARα expression and signaling  [58]
Experimental myocarditis  Balb/c mice  Cocoa polyphenol extract (oral)  3 weeks  ↓ Myocarditis-induced IL-1β, IL-6, E-selectin, VCAM-1, NF-κB  [60]
Atherogenic diet  ApoE3Leiden mice  High flavanol chocolate-enriched diet (8.75%)  20 weeks  ↑ High cholesterol-induced VCAM-1 and E-selectin  [62]
The data presented in Table 1 provides a comprehensive overview of experimental conditions and outcomes related to obesity-
related inflammation in various animal models. The table systematically categorizes samples based on key parameters,
including growth temperature (°C) and flow rates of argon (Ar) and nitrogen (N2). Notably, the N2/Ar flow rate ratio is
highlighted, indicating the specific gas mixture utilized in each experimental setup. This ratio is critical for understanding the
environmental conditions under which the experiments were conducted, as it influences the physiological responses observed in
the subjects. Additionally, the table includes metrics such as the duration of the intervention and the main outcomes associated
with each sample, which are essential for assessing the efficacy of the treatments administered. By documenting the induced
damage alongside the experimental animals used, the table serves as a valuable resource for researchers aiming to replicate or
build upon these findings. Together, these variables elucidate the complex interplay between environmental factors and the
biological mechanisms underlying obesity-related inflammation, offering insights into potential therapeutic strategies.
